tasks.subject: [agent-personality-convention-files] [P1] Add TraitsSchema, ConventionsSchema, and updated request schemas to mesh-schemas.ts
tasks.description:
Add Zod schemas for personality traits and convention file toggles to `packages/shared/src/mesh-schemas.ts`. Also extend `UpdateAgentRequestSchema` to accept the new fields for convention file content.

## Changes to `packages/shared/src/mesh-schemas.ts`

### 1. Add TraitsSchema and ConventionsSchema (after the `EnabledToolGroupsSchema` block, before `AgentManifestSchema`)

```typescript
/** Personality trait levels (1-5 scale, default 3 = Balanced) */
export const TraitsSchema = z
  .object({
    tone: z.number().int().min(1).max(5).default(3),
    autonomy: z.number().int().min(1).max(5).default(3),
    caution: z.number().int().min(1).max(5).default(3),
    communication: z.number().int().min(1).max(5).default(3),
    creativity: z.number().int().min(1).max(5).default(3),
  })
  .openapi('Traits');

export type Traits = z.infer<typeof TraitsSchema>;

/** Convention file injection toggles */
export const ConventionsSchema = z
  .object({
    soul: z.boolean().default(true),
    nope: z.boolean().default(true),
  })
  .openapi('Conventions');

export type Conventions = z.infer<typeof ConventionsSchema>;
```

### 2. Extend AgentManifestSchema

Add two optional fields to the `AgentManifestSchema` object, after the existing `personaEnabled` field:

```typescript
traits: TraitsSchema.optional(),
conventions: ConventionsSchema.optional(),
```

The existing `persona` and `personaEnabled` fields remain for migration compatibility.

### 3. Extend UpdateAgentRequestSchema

The current `UpdateAgentRequestSchema` uses `.pick()` from `AgentManifestSchema`. After adding `traits` and `conventions` to `AgentManifestSchema`, add them to the pick list:

```typescript
export const UpdateAgentRequestSchema = AgentManifestSchema.pick({
  name: true,
  description: true,
  runtime: true,
  capabilities: true,
  behavior: true,
  budget: true,
  namespace: true,
  persona: true,
  personaEnabled: true,
  color: true,
  icon: true,
  enabledToolGroups: true,
  traits: true,       // NEW
  conventions: true,  // NEW
})
  .partial()
  .openapi('UpdateAgentRequest');
```

Also create a separate schema for convention file content updates (used by the PATCH route extension in task 2.2):

```typescript
/** Extended update request body including convention file content */
export const UpdateAgentConventionsSchema = z
  .object({
    soulContent: z.string().max(4000).optional(),
    nopeContent: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
    traits: TraitsSchema.optional(),
    conventions: ConventionsSchema.optional(),
  })
  .openapi('UpdateAgentConventions');

export type UpdateAgentConventions = z.infer<typeof UpdateAgentConventionsSchema>;
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `TraitsSchema` validates 5 integer fields (tone, autonomy, caution, communication, creativity) each 1-5 with default 3
- [ ] `ConventionsSchema` validates 2 boolean fields (soul, nope) each defaulting to true
- [ ] `AgentManifestSchema` includes optional `traits` and `conventions` fields
- [ ] `UpdateAgentRequestSchema` pick list includes `traits` and `conventions`
- [ ] `UpdateAgentConventionsSchema` validates `soulContent` (max 4000), `nopeContent` (max 2000), `traits`, and `conventions`
- [ ] All schemas have `.openapi()` metadata
- [ ] Types are exported: `Traits`, `Conventions`, `UpdateAgentConventions`
- [ ] Existing agent manifests without `traits`/`conventions` still parse successfully (fields are optional)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes across the monorepo
tasks.activeForm: Adding trait and convention schemas to mesh-schemas.ts

tasks.subject: [agent-personality-convention-files] [P1] Add traits_json and conventions_json columns to DB schema
tasks.description:
Add two new text columns to the `agents` table in `packages/db/src/schema/mesh.ts` to store traits and conventions as JSON. These are derived cache columns (file is canonical per ADR-0043).

## Changes to `packages/db/src/schema/mesh.ts`

Add two columns to the `agents` table definition, after the existing `personaEnabled` column:

```typescript
export const agents = sqliteTable('agents', {
  // ... existing columns ...
  persona: text('persona'),
  personaEnabled: integer('persona_enabled', { mode: 'boolean' }).notNull().default(true),
  traitsJson: text('traits_json'),          // NEW: JSON string of Traits
  conventionsJson: text('conventions_json'), // NEW: JSON string of Conventions
  color: text('color'),
  // ... rest of existing columns ...
});
```

Both columns are nullable text (JSON strings). `null` means the agent has no traits/conventions configured (legacy agent).

## Migration considerations

Drizzle handles schema changes. The reconciler (`reconciler.ts`) will need to be updated in the server integration phase (task 2.2) to sync these new fields from disk to DB.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `traits_json` column added to `agents` table as nullable text
- [ ] `conventions_json` column added to `agents` table as nullable text
- [ ] Existing columns are unchanged
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] DB migration works (new columns allow null, so no breaking changes for existing data)
tasks.activeForm: Adding DB columns for traits and conventions

tasks.subject: [agent-personality-convention-files] [P1] Add optional applyConventions method to AgentRuntime interface
tasks.description:
Extend the `AgentRuntime` interface in `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts` with an optional `applyConventions` method for runtime-agnostic personality injection.

## Changes to `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts`

Add the following method to the `AgentRuntime` interface, in a new `// --- Conventions ---` section before the `// --- Lifecycle ---` section:

```typescript
export interface AgentRuntime {
  // ... existing methods ...

  // --- Conventions ---

  /**
   * Apply personality and safety convention content to an agent session.
   * Called by the server before session start. Each runtime implements
   * its own injection mechanism.
   *
   * @param persona - Rendered SOUL.md content (traits + custom prose), or null if disabled
   * @param safetyBoundaries - NOPE.md content, or null if disabled
   * @param agentPath - Absolute path to the agent's working directory
   */
  applyConventions?(
    persona: string | null,
    safetyBoundaries: string | null,
    agentPath: string
  ): Promise<void>;

  // --- Lifecycle ---
  // ... existing methods ...
}
```

The method is optional (marked with `?`) so existing runtime implementations don't break. The Claude Code runtime already handles convention injection through the context builder (task 2.1), so it does not need to implement `applyConventions` immediately. This interface extension is for future runtimes that may use different injection mechanisms.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `applyConventions` method added to `AgentRuntime` interface with `?` (optional)
- [ ] Method signature: `(persona: string | null, safetyBoundaries: string | null, agentPath: string) => Promise<void>`
- [ ] TSDoc comment documents the method's purpose and parameters
- [ ] Existing runtime implementations (ClaudeCodeRuntime, FakeAgentRuntime) are not broken by the addition
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes across the monorepo

[agent-personality-convention-files] [P3] Update AgentDialog to use PersonalityTab and pass convention data
tasks.description:
Update `AgentDialog.tsx` to rename the "Persona" tab to "Personality", import `PersonalityTab` instead of `PersonaTab`, and pass convention file content from the GET response to the new tab. ## Changes to `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/AgentDialog.tsx` ### 1. Update imports Replace: ```typescript import { PersonaTab } from './PersonaTab'; ``` With: ```typescript import { PersonalityTab } from './PersonalityTab'; ``` ### 2. Update sidebar navigation item Change the tab value and label from "persona" / "Persona" to "personality" / "Personality": ```typescript Personality ``` ### 3. Update the tab panel Replace the persona panel with the personality panel. The `handleUpdate` callback needs to be extended to handle convention file content alongside manifest updates: ```typescript
Personality { // Convention file content and manifest fields are sent in the same PATCH updateAgent.mutate({ path: projectPath, updates }); }} onMigrate={() => { // Trigger persona migration via the dedicated endpoint // This could be a fetch call to POST /api/agents/current/migrate-persona?path=... // The response will trigger a re-fetch of agent data }} />
``` Note: The `useCurrentAgent` hook's response type needs to be extended to include `soulContent` and `nopeContent` from the GET response. The `useUpdateAgent` mutation needs to be extended to accept convention file content. These changes should be made in the entity layer hooks (`useCurrentAgent`, `useUpdateAgent`) to pass through the extra fields. ### 4. Update entity layer hooks (if needed) If the `useCurrentAgent` hook's return type doesn't include `soulContent`/`nopeContent`, extend the transport call or response type to include them. The server already returns these fields (task 2.2), so this is primarily a type widening. ### 5. Update the `AgentDialog.test.tsx` Update the test to reference "Personality" instead of "Persona" and verify the new tab renders correctly. ### 6. Update barrel exports In `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/index.ts`, update the exports if `PersonaTab` was exported: - Remove `PersonaTab` export - Add `PersonalityTab` export (if externally used) - Add `PersonalitySliders`, `ConventionFileEditor`, `InjectionPreview` exports ### 7. Delete old files - Delete `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/PersonaTab.tsx` - Delete `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/__tests__/PersonaTab.test.tsx` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Sidebar shows "Personality" instead of "Persona" with same Sparkles icon - [ ] PersonalityTab receives `soulContent` and `nopeContent` from server response - [ ] Tab panel header reads "Personality" - [ ] Update callback sends convention file content to server - [ ] Migration callback triggers the migrate-persona endpoint - [ ] Old `PersonaTab.tsx` file is deleted - [ ] Old `PersonaTab.test.tsx` file is deleted - [ ] Barrel exports updated - [ ] Existing `AgentDialog.test.tsx` updated and passing - [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes

tasks.subject: [agent-personality-convention-files] [P4] End-to-end validation of full personality workflow
tasks.description:
Verify the complete personality workflow end-to-end: create an agent, adjust sliders, edit convention files, verify injection output, and test migration of legacy agents.

## Verification Steps

### 1. Agent Creation Flow

- Start dev server (`pnpm dev`)
- Create a new agent via the UI
- Verify `.dork/SOUL.md` is created with default template (trait markers, identity section, values section)
- Verify `.dork/NOPE.md` is created with default safety rules
- Verify `agent.json` has no `traits` or `conventions` fields initially (they use defaults)

### 2. Personality Configuration Flow

- Open agent settings dialog -> "Personality" tab
- Verify 5 trait sliders are visible, all at level 3 (Balanced)
- Adjust tone slider to level 1 (Silent)
- Verify SOUL.md editor shows updated trait section with "Silent" directive
- Verify injection preview updates to show the new trait
- Edit custom prose in SOUL.md editor (below the trait markers)
- Toggle SOUL.md off -> verify preview omits `<agent_persona>` block
- Toggle SOUL.md on -> verify preview restores `<agent_persona>` block
- Edit NOPE.md content
- Verify NOPE.md advisory disclaimer is visible
- Toggle NOPE.md off -> verify preview omits `<agent_safety_boundaries>` block
- Save changes -> verify files on disk are updated

### 3. Injection Verification

- Open a session for the configured agent
- Send a test message
- Check the system prompt append (via server logs or debug tools) to verify:
  - `<agent_identity>` block is present
  - `<agent_persona>` block contains rendered SOUL.md with current trait values
  - `<agent_safety_boundaries>` block contains NOPE.md content
  - Disabled convention files are not injected

### 4. Legacy Agent Migration

- Create an agent with only the legacy `persona` field (no SOUL.md on disk)
- Open the Personality tab
- Verify migration is triggered: SOUL.md is created with persona text as custom prose
- Verify NOPE.md is scaffolded with defaults
- Verify the legacy `persona` field remains in `agent.json` for rollback safety

### 5. Performance Check

- Measure `buildAgentBlock` execution time with convention files present
- Verify total overhead is under 5ms for 2 file reads + trait rendering
- File sizes: SOUL.md < 4KB, NOPE.md < 2KB

### 6. Non-Regression Checks

- Verify existing agents without SOUL.md/NOPE.md still work (graceful fallback to legacy persona)
- Verify agent settings dialog remains functional for Identity, Capabilities, and Connections tabs
- Verify all existing tests pass: `pnpm test -- --run`
- Verify typecheck passes: `pnpm typecheck`
- Verify lint passes: `pnpm lint`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Complete creation -> configuration -> injection workflow works end-to-end
- [ ] Trait slider changes are reflected in SOUL.md and injection preview in real time
- [ ] Convention file content is correctly saved to disk and loaded from disk
- [ ] Toggle states correctly enable/disable injection blocks
- [ ] Legacy agent migration preserves existing persona text
- [ ] `buildAgentBlock` overhead < 5ms with convention files
- [ ] All existing tests pass (non-regression)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
tasks.activeForm: Running end-to-end validation of personality workflow
